reasoning: Initial state shows x=10.0, x0=0.0, v=5.0, t=2.0. I need to test how x changes with v and t to find the equation. I will increase v to see the effect on x.

In the initial state (from history), x=10.0 when v=5.0 and t=2.0. After increasing v to 10.0 (doubling v), x became 20.0 (doubling x). This suggests x is proportional to v. Since t remained constant at 2.0, I need to test the effect of t. I will increase t to see if x changes proportionally.

From the history, when v doubled (5.0 to 10.0) with t constant, x doubled (10.0 to 20.0), suggesting x ∝ v. When t increased from 2.0 to 3.0 (factor 1.5) with v constant at 10.0, x increased from 20.0 to 30.0 (factor 1.5), suggesting x ∝ t. The relationship appears to be x = v * t. Since x0 is always 0.0, it doesn't affect the result. I have enough evidence to conclude the equation.
